name: flask-auth

metadata.name: Flask-Auth
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
==========
Flask-Auth
==========

Database-agnostic extension for Flask to support role-based authentication of
users.

Links
-----

* `Documentation <http://packages.python.org/Flask-Auth>`_
* `Repository <http://bitbucket.org/Shotca/flask-auth/>`_
* `Development version
  <http://bitbucket.org/Shotca/flask-auth/get/tip.gz#egg=Flask-Auth-dev>`_

Revisions
---------
0.85
````
* Updated to Flask 0.8 new naming scheme of using flask.ext for sqlalchemy.

* Fixed issue with importing sqlalchemy models.

0.8
```
Major revision.

* Not storing the entire user in the session anymore (which was convenient but bad practice) but only its __dict__.

* As a result of the above, the get_current_user call is now gone and replaced by get_current_user_data call. Similar functionality to the original call can now be obtained by doing AuthUser.load_current_user().

* Created SQLAlchemy plug-and-play model using constructor.

* Small bugfixes.

0.7
```
Added tests, permission example, bunch of bugfixes.

0.6
```
Added permission model, added examples, first release on PyPI.

0.1 - 0.5
`````````
Logging in, session management, encryption, etc.

modules.name: setup
documentation:
==========
Flask-Auth
==========

Database-agnostic extension for Flask to support role-based authentication of
users.

Links
-----

* `Documentation <http://packages.python.org/Flask-Auth>`_
* `Repository <http://bitbucket.org/Shotca/flask-auth/>`_
* `Development version
  <http://bitbucket.org/Shotca/flask-auth/get/tip.gz#egg=Flask-Auth-dev>`_

Revisions
---------
0.85
```
* Updated to flask 0.8 new naming scheme of using flask.ext instead of flaskext.

* Fixed issue with importing sqlalchemy models.

0.8
```
Mayor revision.

* Not storing the entire user in the session anymore (which was convenient 
but bad practice) but only its __dict__.

* As a result of the above, the get_current_user call is now gone and 
replaced by get_current_user_data call. Similar functionality to the original
call can now be obtained by doing AuthUser.load_current_user().

* Created SQLAlchemy plug-and-play model using constructor.

* Small bugfixes.

0.7
```
Added tests, permission example, bunch of bugfixes.

0.6
```
Added permission model, added examples, first release on PyPI.

0.1 - 0.5
`````````
Logging in, session management, encryption, etc.

modules.name: flaskext.auth.models.gae
documentation:
Module to provide plug-and-play authentication support for Google App Engine
using flask-auth.

classes.name: flaskext.auth.models.gae.User
documentation: Implementation of User for persistence in Google's App Engine datastore.

modules.name: flaskext.auth
documentation:
flaskext.auth
~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Flask extension for role-based user administration and authentication. 
Designed to be DB agnostic and still fairly plug-and-play.

:copyright: (c) 2012 by Lars de Ridder.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: flaskext.auth.models.sa
documentation: Module to provide plug-and-play authentication support for SQLAlchemy.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.models.sa.get_user_class
documentation:
Factory function to create an SQLAlchemy User model with a declarative 
base (for example db.Model from the Flask-SQLAlchemy extension).

modules.name: flaskext.auth.permissions
documentation: Module containing functions and classes specific to the permission model.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.permissions.has_permission
documentation: Function to check if a user has the specified permission.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.permissions.permission_required
documentation:
Decorator for views that require a certain permission of the logged in 
user.

classes.name: flaskext.auth.permissions.Permission
documentation:
Permission object, representing actions that can be taken on a resource.

Attributes:

- resource: A resource is a component on which actions can be performed.
  Examples: post, user, ticket, product, but also post.comment, user.role,
  etc.
- action: Any action that can be performed on a resource. Names of actions
  should be short and clear. Examples: create, read, update, delete, download,
  list, etc.

classes.name: flaskext.auth.permissions.Role
documentation:
Role object to group users and permissions.

Attributes:

- name: The name of the role.
- permissions: A list of permissions.

functions.name: examples.no_db_persistence.init_users
documentation:
Initializing users by hardcoding password. Another use case is to read
usernames from an external file (like /etc/passwd).

functions.name: examples.permissions.load_role
documentation:
Function that has to be defined to be able to retrieve the actual role
object from the user.role attribute. In this simple case, we could
actually assign the role object directly to user.role, in which this
function would simply be the identity function (lambda x: x). This extra
step becomes needed however in case the role object is more complex
and it can't be simply pickled anymore.

functions.name: examples.permissions.init_users
documentation:
Initializing users by hardcoding password. Another use case is to read
usernames from an external file (like /etc/passwd).

modules.name: flaskext.auth.auth
documentation:
Base module of the extension. Contains basic functions, the Auth object and the
AuthUser base class.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.auth.encrypt
documentation: Encrypts a password based on the hashing algorithm.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.auth.login
documentation:
Logs the user in. Note that NO AUTHENTICATION is done by this function. If
you want to authenticate a user, use the AuthUser.authenticate() method.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.auth.logout
documentation: Logs the currently logged in user out and returns the user data.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.auth.get_current_user_data
documentation:
Returns the data of the current user (user.__dict__) if there is a
current user and he didn't time out yet. If timeout should be ignored,
provide apply_timeout=False.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.auth.not_logged_in
documentation: Executes not logged in callback. Not for external use.

functions.name: flaskext.auth.auth.login_required
documentation:
Decorator for views that require login. Callback can be specified to
override the default callback on the auth object.

classes.name: flaskext.auth.auth.Auth
documentation:
Extension initialization object containing settings for the extension.

Supported settings:

- login_url_name: Name of the URL that is used for login. It's used in
  the not_logged_in_callback if provided in the constructor.
- not_logged_in_callback: Function to call when a user accesses a page
  without being logged in. Normally used to redirect to the login page.
  If a login_url_name is provided, it will by default redirect to that
  url. Otherwise, the default is abort(401).
- not_permitted_callback: Function to call when a user tries to access
  a page for which he doesn't have the permission. Default: abort(401).
- hash_algorithm: Algorithm from the hashlib library used for password
  encryption. Default: sha1.
- user_timeout: Timeout (in seconds) after which the sesion of the user
  expires. Default: 3600. A timeout of 0 means it will never expire.
- load_role: Function to load a role. Is called with user.role as only
  parameter.

classes.name: flaskext.auth.auth.AuthUser

methods.name: flaskext.auth.auth.AuthUser.set_and_encrypt_password
documentation:
Encrypts and sets the password. If no salt is provided, a new
one is generated.

methods.name: flaskext.auth.auth.AuthUser.authenticate
documentation:
Attempts to verify the password and log the user in. Returns true if 
succesful.

methods.name: flaskext.auth.auth.AuthUser.load_current_user
documentation: Load current user based on the result of get_current_user_data().

Baseclass for a user model. Contains a few convenience methods.

Attributes:

- username: Username of the user.
- password: Password of the user. By default not encrypted. The 
  set_and_encrypt_password() method sets and encrypts the password.
- salt: Salt used for the encrytion of the password.
- role: Role of this user.
